segments.text: How you have helped the powerless! How you have saved the arm that is feeble!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: What advice you have offered to one without wisdom! And what great insight you have displayed!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Who has helped you utter these words? And whose spirit spoke from your mouth?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The dead are in deep anguish, those beneath the waters and all that live in them.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The realm of the dead is naked before God; Destruction lies uncovered.
He spreads out the northern skies over empty space; he suspends the earth over nothing.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He wraps up the waters in his clouds, yet the clouds do not burst under their weight.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He covers the face of the full moon, spreading his clouds over it.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He marks out the horizon on the face of the waters for a boundary between light and darkness.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The pillars of the heavens quake, aghast at his rebuke.
By his power he churned up the sea; by his wisdom he cut Rahab to pieces.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: By his breath the skies became fair; his hand pierced the gliding serpent.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And these are but the outer fringe of his works; how faint the whisper we hear of him! Who then can understand the thunder of his power?
segments.speaker: Job
